channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Na prisão há seis meses, Bolsonaro planeja vingança pela condenação
item.description: Jair Bolsonaro, imprisoned for six months for crimes against the Constitution, is strategically planning the 2026 election. His main objective is to secure political positions for his family and ensure 42 loyal votes in the Senate to pressure the STF with impeachment requests. His son Flávio Bolsonaro was named as the PL's presidential candidate, surprising allies and positioning the Bolsonaro family competitively despite the senator's absence from campaigning.

item.title: Ex-marido de Maria da Penha e mais 3 viram réus por campanha de ódio contra a ativista
item.description: Four people, including Maria da Penha's ex-husband, have become defendants for participating in a hate campaign against the activist. They are accused of using fake news, forged reports, and online harassment to attack Maria da Penha's reputation and discredit the law named after her.

item.title: TSE retoma nesta terça julgamento de recursos que pedem cassação de mandato e inelegibilidade de Cláudio Castro
item.description: The TSE resumes judgment on appeals requesting the annulment of the mandate and ineligibility of the Governor of Rio de Janeiro, Cláudio Castro, for alleged electoral irregularities in 2022. The accusations involve abuse of political and economic power, improper use of Ceperj and Uerj, and creation of social programs not provided for in the budget.

item.title: PSD aposta em reação de Tarcísio a pressão do PL para seguir com vaga de vice
item.description: The PSD is counting on Tarcísio de Freitas's reaction to PL pressure to keep Felício Ramuth as his running mate. The party hopes the governor will resist Valdemar Costa Neto and PL deputies' insistence on nominating André do Prado for the position.

item.title: STF julga deputados do PL acusados de pedirem ‘pedágio’ de R$ 1,6 mi em emendas do orçamento secreto
item.description: STF begins trial of PL deputies accused of demanding R$1.6 million bribe in secret budget amendments. Josimar Maranhãozinho, Pastor Gil and Bosco Costa are charged with criminal organization and passive corruption. The scheme involved R$6.67 million in parliamentary amendments.

item.title: Indefinição sobre TCU indica que nome do PT não tem votos garantidos
item.description: Uncertainty about the date of the TCU minister election indicates that Odair Cunha (PT-MG), despite being the favorite, doesn't yet have guaranteed votes. The outcome depends on government and candidate negotiation skills, plus support from center and right-wing parties. Multiple candidates may benefit the PT member by splitting opposition votes.

item.title: El fuego amigo contra el PT marca el debate legislativo de la reforma electoral
item.description: The article discusses internal disputes within Mexico's ruling coalition over President Claudia Sheinbaum's proposed political-electoral reform. The Labor Party (PT) accuses Morena of conducting a negative campaign against them for their resistance to supporting the reform, which seeks to redesign Mexico's political system. The reform faces difficulties in being approved without the support of PT and PVEM allies.

item.title: Boric, a un día de dejar La Moneda: “Ser presidente es como tener la cordillera en la espalda; algo hermoso, pero muy pesado”
item.description: In his final interview before leaving the Chilean presidency, Gabriel Boric spoke about his experience as head of state, comparing it to 'having the Andes on his back.' During Don Francisco's program, he discussed his personal life, including recent fatherhood with his daughter Violeta and his relationship with Paula Carrasco.

item.title: Javier Milei en Nueva York: “Soy el presidente más sionista del mundo”
item.description: Argentine President Javier Milei declared himself 'the most Zionist president in the world' during an event in New York, explicitly aligning with Israel amid Middle East military escalation. On his 15th US visit in two years, Milei reaffirmed his pro-Israel stance, announced an April trip to Tel Aviv, and maintained plans despite ongoing conflict.

item.title: Paz consigue una pasajera estabilidad económica en Bolivia evitando al Parlamento
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz has stabilized the economy by eliminating fuel subsidies, despite an 80% increase in gasoline prices. However, analysts warn this relief is temporary, as structural reforms needed require parliamentary approval, which the government has been avoiding through decrees.

item.title: Aislados pero cerca de sus raíces: las naciones de la Amazonia boliviana trazan planes de vida para evitar su extinción
item.description: Indigenous communities in the Bolivian Amazon face geographical isolation and difficulties accessing basic services, yet maintain strong cultural identity and self-governance. With support from Spanish Cooperation, they are developing community life plans to organize demands and increase their influence with the State. The project involves community diagnostics to improve relations with local and municipal authorities.

item.title: Análise: classificação de CV e PCC como organizações terroristas pelos EUA vira armadilha eleitoral para 2026
item.description: The potential US classification of Comando Vermelho and PCC as terrorist organizations could become a controversial issue in the 2026 elections, with opposition accusing the government of 'protecting' criminal factions. The discussion reveals a political trap where legal explanations about the difference between terrorism and organized crime may be distorted in the electoral debate.

item.title: La carrera por la IA impulsa el comercio global a niveles récord pese a las tensiones geopolíticas
item.description: Global trade reached record levels in 2025, driven by the AI infrastructure race and reconfiguration of Chinese exports. Despite declining US-China trade to just 2% of global commerce, the global connectivity index remained stable at 25%. Companies anticipated imports before tariffs and redirected exports to new markets.

item.title: La liberación de los mariachis Gámez-Cuéllar: el caso que hizo a los republicanos alzar la voz en contra de la política migratoria de Trump
item.description: The release of the Gámez-Cuéllar family, three young mariachi musicians and their parents detained by ICE in Texas, exposed cracks in Trump's immigration policy and led Latino voices within the Republican Party to openly question migrant persecution. Following pressure from lawmakers of both parties, the family was freed, with Republicans like Mónica de la Cruz claiming credit for the action.

item.title: Relação de Vorcaro com políticos arrasta nomes de direita e esquerda para escândalo do Banco Master
item.description: The Master Bank scandal involves politicians from both right and left, including Ciro Nogueira, Davi Alcolumbre, Jair Bolsonaro, Lula, and Tarcísio de Freitas. Daniel Vorcaro, the arrested ex-banker, maintained relationships with various political leaders, with accusations of favoritism and legislative proposals that would benefit the bank.

item.title: Datafolha: 9% veem corrupção como principal problema do país em meio a casos Master e INSS
item.description: Datafolha poll shows 9% of Brazilians see corruption as the main national problem, despite Master Bank and INSS scandals. Health (21%) and security (19%) top concerns, followed by economy (11%) and education (9%). The survey interviewed 2,004 people before revelations about messages between STF minister and former banker.

item.title: Eduardo Leite destoa de Caiado e Ratinho e sobe o tom contra bolsonarismo no PSD
item.description: Eduardo Leite has distinguished himself from other PSD presidential hopefuls, Ronaldo Caiado and Ratinho Jr., by adopting a more critical stance toward bolsonarismo. While Leite criticizes the possibility of replacing PT with a 'bad manager' like Bolsonaro, Caiado and Ratinho Jr. maintain positions more aligned with bolsonarismo, having participated in pro-Bolsonaro demonstrations and against the January 8, 2023 arrests.

item.title: STF tende a condenar deputados do PL e sinalizar ao Congresso rigor com desvio de emendas
item.description: The STF's First Panel tends to convict three PL deputies accused of diverting parliamentary amendments, establishing severe penalties and signaling to Congress a strict stance against this practice. The trial of deputies Josimar Maranhãozinho, Pastor Gil and Bosco Costa, accused of passive corruption and criminal organization, begins this Tuesday and may extend until Wednesday. The case is considered a thermometer of the STF's position on amendment diversions, being the first submitted to collegiate analysis since Minister Flávio Dino's offensive for more transparency.

item.title: Datafolha: Maioria dos brasileiros se informa sobre política por programas de TV e redes sociais
item.description: Datafolha survey shows most Brazilians get political information from TV (58%) and social media (54%). Only 3% don't use any information sources. Among Lula voters, TV dominates, while for Bolsonaro supporters, social media leads.

item.title: Comissão do Senado cancela sessão que ouviria Vorcaro nesta terça
item.description: The Senate's Economic Affairs Committee (CAE) canceled the session that would hear banker Daniel Vorcaro, owner of Banco Master, who was imprisoned in Brasília. Vorcaro was not required to attend the hearing, and the meeting had been rescheduled previously.

item.title: Ex-marido de Maria da Penha e mais 3 viram réus por campanha de ódio
item.description: The Ceará Justice accepted the MP's accusation against Maria da Penha's ex-husband and three other men for a hate campaign. They are accused of attacking the activist's honor, spreading fake news, and using a forged report to discredit the Maria da Penha Law.

item.title: Com Haddad, favoritismo de Tarcísio vai ruir, diz deputado petista
item.description: Petista deputy Rui Falcão claims that Fernando Haddad's candidacy for governor of São Paulo will quickly erode Governor Tarcísio de Freitas's favoritism. Falcão highlights that Haddad will be a competent and honest opponent, contrasting with Tarcísio's government, which he says is a record holder in femicide and toll rates.

item.title: Senador Alessandro Vieira protocola pedido para criar CPI que investigue relação de Alexandre de Moraes e Dias Toffoli com Vorcaro
item.description: Senator Alessandro Vieira filed a request to create a CPI to investigate the relationships between STF ministers Alexandre de Moraes and Dias Toffoli with banker Daniel Vorcaro. The request has 35 signatures, exceeding the minimum of 27 required. The CPI aims to investigate whether the ministers maintained relationships incompatible with the impartial exercise of their functions.

item.title: Lula e presidenta do México discutem cooperação na área de energia
item.description: President Lula and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum discussed strengthening economic and diplomatic ties, focusing on energy cooperation. Sheinbaum is expected to visit Brazil in the second half of the year, when a joint business event is planned.

item.title: Lula e presidenta do México querem reunir empresários dos dois países
item.description: President Lula and Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum agreed to organize an event with business leaders from both countries between June and July to explore new business opportunities. They also discussed deepening bilateral partnership in the energy sector and Lula reiterated his invitation for the Mexican president to visit Brazil.

item.title: Kassab diz que decisão sobre o candidato à Presidência pelo PSD deve sair até 31 de março
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab has moved up the decision on the party's 2026 presidential candidate to March 31st. The three potential candidates - Eduardo Leite, Ratinho Júnior, and Ronaldo Caiado - downplayed recent poll results showing Flavio Bolsonaro tied with Lula.

item.title: Alessandro Vieira e Eduardo Bolsonaro discutem nas redes por causa de CPI contra Moraes e Toffoli
item.description: Senator Alessandro Vieira and former deputy Eduardo Bolsonaro exchanged accusations on social media regarding the creation of a CPI to investigate STF ministers. Vieira accused Eduardo and his brother Flávio Bolsonaro of resisting proceedings against Moraes and Toffoli, while Eduardo called Vieira a 'very dangerous senator'.

item.title: CDH pode votar aumento de penas para discriminação em estádios de futebol
item.description: The Senate Human Rights Committee (CDH) may vote on a bill increasing penalties for discrimination in football stadiums, including racist, xenophobic and homophobic acts. Another proposal adds misogyny as a crime in the Caó Law. The proposals aim to combat discrimination in sporting events and expand protection against hate crimes.

item.title: PT faz jantar de arrecadação com convites a R$ 1.000 e presença esperada de Lula
item.description: The PT will hold a fundraising dinner on April 6 with tickets up to R$ 1,000, expecting President Lula and ministers like Fernando Haddad and Camilo Santana to attend. The event officially celebrates the party's 46th anniversary and aims to strengthen campaign funds.

item.title: A polícia senta praça na política
item.description: The article discusses how recent political scandals, particularly involving former banker Daniel Vorcaro and INSS fraud, have shifted the electoral campaign focus from traditional public safety to corruption among the powerful. Police and professional journalism are working to prevent attempts to halt investigations, affecting figures from both right and left. The initial narrative about combating classic crime has been diverted by these corruption scandals involving important political figures.
